Get support, find answers, and understan: Get support, find answers, and understand logistics terminology
Our support team is available 24/7 via t: Our support team is available 24/7 via the Live Chat widget in the bottom right corner.
How is the \"Days of Supply\" calculated: How is the "Days of Supply" calculated?
Days of Supply is calculated by dividing: Days of Supply is calculated by dividing your current "On-Hand Pallet Inventory" by your "Weekly Avg Daily Usage". This helps predict how long your current stock will last before replenishment is needed.
How do I create a New Delivery Note?: How do I create a New Delivery Note?
Click on \"Transaction\" in the main men: Click on "Transaction" in the main menu, then select "Delivery Monitoring". On the Delivery Monitoring page, click the "New Delivery Note" button. Fill in all required fields including customer, location, pallet type, quantity, and logistic company details, then click "Create Delivery Note".
What does the \"High Risk\" Stock Level: What does the "High Risk" Stock Level alert mean?
A \"High Risk\" alert indicates that you: A "High Risk" alert indicates that your pallet inventory has dropped below 4 days of supply. This means you should immediately arrange for stock replenishment to avoid potential shortages that could impact your operations.
Where can I view my monthly invoices?: Where can I view my monthly invoices?
Go to \"Finance\" menu and select \"Invo: Go to "Finance" menu and select "Invoice". You can view, download, and track all your invoices there. You can also filter by date range, status, or customer to find specific invoices.
How do I report damaged pallets?: How do I report damaged pallets?
When creating or editing a delivery note: When creating or editing a delivery note, you can specify the quantity of damaged pallets in the "Damaged Quantity" field. You can also add notes describing the damage. For urgent issues, please contact our support team directly via phone or email.
Customer-Liable Pallets are the total nu: Customer-Liable Pallets are the total number of pallets currently chargeable to your account. This includes pallets in your on-hand inventory and pallets delivered to your customers that are still within the agreed liability transition period.
A document that accompanies a shipment o: A document that accompanies a shipment of goods and provides details about the delivery, including items, quantities, and destination.
A document confirming that goods have be: A document confirming that goods have been received at their destination, matching them against the original purchase order or delivery note.
The expected date and time when a shipme: The expected date and time when a shipment will leave its origin location or warehouse.
The expected date and time when a shipment will arrive at its destination.
The total quantity of pallets currently: The total quantity of pallets currently available in your warehouses and storage facilities.
Pallets that are currently being transpo: Pallets that are currently being transported between locations, including both incoming and outgoing shipments.
A system where pallets are shared among: A system where pallets are shared among multiple users, reducing costs and improving efficiency in the supply chain.
The flow of pallets in and out of invent: The flow of pallets in and out of inventory, tracked through delivery notes, returns, and transfers.
The inventory level at which a new order: The inventory level at which a new order should be placed to replenish stock before it runs out.
A metric indicating how many days the cu: A metric indicating how many days the current inventory will last based on average daily usage.
A third-party provider that handles the: A third-party provider that handles the transportation and delivery of pallets between locations.
A contract between parties outlining the: A contract between parties outlining the terms of pallet rental, including quantities, pricing, and liability periods.
Move pallets smarter, reduce handling co: Move pallets smarter, reduce handling costs, and accelerate your supply chain.
Sign in to your account to continue: Sign in to your account to continue
